title: qa-concurrent-write-ux: map jj internal error to typed concurrent_write / slug_collision
body:
# Goal

Translate jj's internal "Concurrent checkout" error into
a clean, actionable jjforge typed error. Today the user
sees a 12-line vomit including jj internals, a commit
SHA, and the word "Internal error" — all because a
sibling agent landed a write first.

End state: a concurrent-write conflict produces a
typed `concurrent_write` (or `slug_collision` if the
content allows the distinction) error with a one-line
text rendering and a clean JSON envelope.

# Why now

QA red-team round 2026-06-23 (see "qa-redteam" handoff):

Test 16 (two parallel `jjf new` calls trying to claim
the same slug):

```
jjf: jj cli: `jj new bookmarks(issues) -m jjf: issue 0ca64a7 - create
Jjf-Op: create
Jjf-Issue: 0ca64a7
Jjf-At: 2026-06-23T15:22:06.901486000Z
Jjf-Title: winner B
Jjf-Status: open
Jjf-Op: set-slug
Jjf-Issue: 0ca64a7
Jjf-At: 2026-06-23T15:22:06.901486000Z
Jjf-Slug: race-slot
` exited Some(255):
Internal error: Failed to check out commit c50c7d819aa507052461627b908757c5dd362b63
Caused by: Concurrent checkout
```

The race-safety GUARANTEE is correct (only one writer
landed; bookmark is consistent). But the operator sees
"Internal error" and has no idea what to do. In an
agent-driven loop where parallel writes are the design,
this is the dominant error shape.

# Scope

## Storage

- In the layer that shells out to `jj new` / `jj commit`,
  catch the stderr-string match for "Concurrent checkout"
  / "Failed to check out commit". Translate to
  `Error::ConcurrentWrite { hint: String }`.
- If the failed commit was a create with a slug claim,
  AND the live bookmark now has that slug, the more
  specific `Error::SlugCollision { slug, taken_by: id }`
  is preferred. (Probe via `Storage::resolve(slug)`
  after the failure.)
- Add automatic ONE retry on `ConcurrentWrite` for
  non-slug-claim mutations (comments, updates,
  status changes). The retry uses the same op message
  but with a fresh head-commit. Slug-claim retries
  would race indefinitely — fail fast there.

## CLI

- Render `concurrent_write` as a single line:
  `jjf: concurrent write conflict; another writer
  landed first. Retry your command.` (or the
  hint-text from the storage layer).
- Render `slug_collision` as:
  `jjf: slug "<slug>" already taken by <other-id>.
  Pick a different --slug.`
- Both error kinds documented in `docs/cli-json.md`
  with their envelope.

## Tests

- A concurrent-write test that spawns two threads (or
  `Command::new(...)`s two processes) racing the same
  slug, asserts exactly one succeeds and the loser
  gets the typed `slug_collision` error.
- A concurrent-comment test that spawns two threads
  appending comments to the same issue, asserts BOTH
  comments land (thanks to the automatic retry).

# Acceptance

- [ ] `Error::ConcurrentWrite` and `Error::SlugCollision`
  exist as typed variants.
- [ ] CLI renders both as one-line text (not a stack
  trace).
- [ ] `--json` envelope kinds documented.
- [ ] Auto-retry on `ConcurrentWrite` for non-slug
  mutations. One retry max — if both attempts race,
  surface the typed error.
- [ ] Concurrent-write integration test demonstrating
  the typed error.
- [ ] Concurrent-comment integration test demonstrating
  BOTH comments land after auto-retry.
- [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` stays green.

# Out of scope

- Optimistic locking semantics beyond what jj already
  enforces. We're just translating the existing
  conflict into a clean error.
- A global retry loop with exponential backoff.
  One retry is the v1 policy; revisit if real
  multi-agent loads show contention.
- Detecting concurrent-write on memory mutations
  (`jjf remember`). Memories are scalars; whoever
  writes last wins per the LWW resolver. No retry
  needed.

# Pointers

- Concurrent-checkout error site:
  `crates/jjf-storage/src/jj.rs` (where `jj new` is
  shelled out — search for `Concurrent checkout` or
  the surrounding context).
- Error enum: `crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs` `Error`.
- CLI error renderer:
  `crates/jjf/src/main.rs` (search for `Error::` or
  the `output_error` helper).
- Spec ref: `docs/cli-json.md` for envelope shapes.
- Repro: QA round 2026-06-23 tests 16, 23.
